touches.ai_summary: Mia Ryan submitted an inbound request through the website expressing interest in automation tools for sales-to-CS handoff. Brief form fill mentioned current manual processes causing delays in customer onboarding.

Qualification call with Mia Ryan to understand OrbitalAgency's current handoff process. Mia described pain points with scattered information across tools and CS team waiting days for context. Currently evaluating solutions including Fathom. Discussed team size (61 sales/CS seats) and basic use case fit.

questions_asked.question: How does your platform handle technical call transcription for cybersecurity discussions?
questions_asked.answer: Our AI is trained on technical vocabulary including security terms, with custom glossaries available for specialized terminology.

questions_asked.question: What's your current timeline for making a decision?
questions_asked.answer: Hoping to have something in place within 60 days, but need to evaluate options first.

questions_asked.question: Does this integrate with Salesforce and Zendesk?

touches.ai_summary: Brief discovery call to dive deeper into OrbitalAgency's tech stack and workflow. Mia confirmed they're using Salesforce, Zendesk, and Slack. Discussed specific pain point: CS team missing critical context from sales calls, leading to repeated questions and frustrated enterprise customers.

questions_asked.question: Who else should be involved in evaluating this solution?

touches.ai_summary: Discovery call focusing on specific automation requirements. Mia detailed their ideal workflow: calls automatically transcribed, action items extracted, key deal info pushed to Salesforce, and CS onboarding packet auto-generated. Discussed unlimited automation tier as best fit for their volume (300+ calls/month).

questions_asked.question: Can we customize what information gets pulled into the CS handoff packet?
questions_asked.answer: Absolutely. You can create custom templates and rules for what data gets extracted and where it flows.

questions_asked.question: What's the biggest risk to this deal not closing?
questions_asked.answer: Honestly, if Fathom's price is significantly lower and Jack pushes back on budget, that could be an issue. But I think we can make the ROI case.

next_steps: Invite Jordan and Avery to demo

touches.ai_summary: Email confirming rescheduled demo time and sending calendar invite to Mia, Jordan Wilson, and Avery Weiss. Mia confirmed all three will attend.

Product demo with Mia, Jordan, and Avery. Walked through live example of sales call transcription, automated action item extraction, and Salesforce sync. Jordan asked detailed questions about mobile app functionality. Avery focused on technical implementation and API access. Team seemed engaged but Avery raised concerns about data security for customer calls.

questions_asked.question: How does the mobile app work for field sales reps?
questions_asked.answer: Mobile app captures calls, transcribes in real-time, and syncs to CRM. Works on iOS and Android with offline capability.

questions_asked.question: What are your data security certifications?
questions_asked.answer: We're SOC 2 Type II certified, GDPR compliant, and offer data residency options. All transcripts encrypted at rest and in transit.

questions_asked.question: What would make this a clear yes for your team?
questions_asked.answer: If we can validate the ROI and get comfortable with security, this could work. Need to show Jack the business case.

objections: Need more detail on data security protocols for customer call recordings
next_steps: Send security documentation and compliance certifications

touches.ai_summary: Technical demo and security deep-dive with Avery. Reviewed encryption standards, access controls, and compliance frameworks. Walked through API documentation and discussed custom integration possibilities. Avery seemed satisfied with security posture but mentioned they'd need to review with IT security team.

questions_asked.question: Can we control who has access to specific call recordings?
questions_asked.answer: Yes, role-based access controls let you define permissions at the team, user, and even call level.

questions_asked.question: What's your uptime SLA?
questions_asked.answer: 99.9% uptime SLA with redundant infrastructure and 24/7 monitoring. We provide monthly uptime reports.

questions_asked.question: Any technical blockers from your perspective?
questions_asked.answer: Not blockers, but I need to validate the API integration with our Salesforce customizations. I'll test that this week.

next_steps: Provide API sandbox access for Avery

Mia indicated price is higher than Fathom but believes she can justify it based on automation capabilities and ROI. Needs to present business case to Jack.

Mia advocating for 15-20% discount to close the gap. Discussed potential concessions: annual payment upfront, case study participation, or tiered rollout.

questions_asked.question: Can you match Fathom's pricing?

objections: Jack wants pricing closer to competitive alternative
Considering phased rollout to reduce upfront cost

touches.ai_summary: Follow-up email with three pricing scenarios: 10% discount for annual prepay, 12% discount with case study commitment, or phased rollout starting with 35 seats. Also included feature comparison matrix vs Fathom to reinforce value gap. Mia responded favoring the 12% discount option and will discuss with Jack.

next_steps: Mia to review options with Jack

touches.ai_summary: Executive meeting with Jack Kapoor, Mia Ryan, and rep to review final proposal and business case. Walked through ROI calculation showing 200+ hours saved monthly in handoff time.
